title: Logical AND &&
subtitle: Both conditions must be true
goal: Learn to use the && operator to check multiple conditions at the same time.
explanation: It returns true only when both sides are true.
If at least one side is false, the result is false.
&& is often used in checks where several conditions must be met at the same time.
This operator is very important for if statements and program logic.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine a door with two requirements: you need a key and permission. If you have only one of them, the door will not open.

remember: && returns true only if both sides are true.
If one side is false, the result is false.
&& is used for multiple required conditions.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners sometimes expect true when at least one condition is true. But with &&, every condition must be true.
